content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 6, 1992

Bradley W. Hertz
Attorney at Law
15223 Magnolia Blvd., Suite A
Sherman Oaks, CA 91403

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-92-40

Dear Mr. Hertz:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice under
the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act")1/
and confirming the advice I gave you by telephone. Because your request
is a general inquiry, we treat it as one for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTIONS

1. Must the name of a controlled committee include the
name of the controlling officeholder?

2. Must a committee primarily formed to oppose the recall
of an elected official include in references to the
committee a statement that the committee is opposed
to the recall?

3. Is the Form 419 the correct campaign disclosure form to
be filed by a controlled committee primarily formed to
oppose a recall?

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Bradley Hertz
I-92-40
Page 2

CONCLUSIONS

1. A controlled committee is not required to include the
name of the controlling officeholder as part of the name
of the committee. However, a controlling candidate's name must be included in the identification of
the sender on all mass mailings sent by the committee.

2. A committee primarily formed to oppose the recall of an
elected official is required to include in any reference
to the committee required by law a statement that the
committee is opposed to the recall.

3. The Form 419 is the correct campaign disclosure form to
be filed by a committee primarily formed to oppose a
recall, including such committees that are controlled
by a candidate or officeholder.

ANALYSIS

In June 1988, the voters of California adopted Proposition 68
which amended the Act. One of the provisions of Proposition 68
was a requirement that the name of a committee shall include or be
accompanied by the name of any person by which the committee is
controlled. On November 1, 1990, the California Supreme Court
issued its decision in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v.
Fair Political Practices Commission (1990) 51 Cal.3d 744, and this
decision became final on January 18, 1991. The Supreme Court held
that all of the provisions of Proposition 68 were invalid.

Consequently, the Act does not include any requirement that
the name of a controlled committee must include the name of the
person who controls the committee. However, please note that the
Act does require a controlled committee that is the sender of a
mass mailing to include on the outside of the mailing the name of
the person controlling the committee, in addition to other
required information. (Section 84305.)

Your second question concerns the requirement for a ballot
measure committee to identify the measure it supports or opposes.
Section 84107 states:

Within 30 days of the designation of the numerical
order of propositions appearing on the ballot, any
committee which has as its principal activity the
support of or opposition to a ballot measure, shall, if
supporting the measure, include the statement, "a
committee for Proposition ___," or, if opposing the
measure, include the statement, "a committee against
Proposition ___," in any reference to the committee
required by law.

Bradley Hertz
I-92-40
Page 3

     The committee you represent is formed primarily to oppose the
recall of a local elected officer.  There is no ballot number or
letter "designation" for the recall measure.  In this situation,
the committee will comply with Section 84107, and the purpose of
Section 84107 will be accomplished, by including in the statement, "a
committee against the recall of (name of officeholder)" in any
reference to the committee required by law.

     In situations where there is no "designation of the numerical
order of propositions appearing on the ballot," an issue arises
concerning the point in time at which Section 84107 applies.  As a
practical matter, it is suggested that the language quoted above
be included in the committee's name when the committee files its
Statement of Organization (Form 410).

     In the case of a committee supporting or opposing the
qualification of a measure which changes to committee supporting
or opposing the passage of a measure, we suggest that the language
required by Section 84107 be included in the committee's name when
it amends its Statement of Organization to change the description
of the nature of its activities.

     In any event, when there is no ballot number or letter
designation for a measure, the language required by Section 84107
must be included in all references to the committee required by
the Act commencing no later than 30 days after the date the
election is called (i.e., the date the governing body adopts a
resolution setting the election date).

     Finally, you asked if Form 419 is the correct campaign
disclosure form to be filed by a committee primarily formed to
oppose the recall of an elected official.  The Act defines
"measure" to include a recall.  (Section 82043.)  Consequently,
Form 419--Ballot Measure Committee Campaign Statement, is the
correct form to be used by the committee.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Carla Wardlow
                                             Assistant Division Chief
                                             Technical Assistance and
                                             Analysis Division
